nonBlockingIssues.description: The `<span role="button">` element lacks focus-visible styling. Native buttons get focus-visible:ring from .btn class in global.css, but custom span[role=button] elements don't have equivalent focus indication. This is a minor accessibility UX gap.

appliedUpdates.description: Added three mobile/convention patterns: (1) Dropdown positioning in scroll containers using fixed positioning with getBoundingClientRect(), (2) Filter persistence via URL search params for back-navigation context, (3) Nested interactive elements using span[role=button] with keyboard handlers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update 'Write or update failing tests first' guidance to acknowledge that for additive CSS-only responsive changes (e.g., adding sm:hidden/hidden sm:block classes), there are no failing tests to write first since the change is additive rather than behavioral. Tests should be written alongside implementation in these cases.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dealer-sales-mobile-navigation-and-lists worker noted in skillFeedback: 'The skill says to write or update failing tests first — in practice, for responsive CSS changes (adding sm:hidden/hidden sm:block), there are no failing tests to write first since the change is additive.'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance about checking global.css for focus-visible styles when implementing span[role=button] patterns. Consider adding a global CSS rule for [role="button"]:focus-visible to ensure consistent focus indication across all interactive elements.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dealer-sales-mobile-dealer-combobox-dom-nesting-fix found that global.css has focus-visible:ring-2 for .btn class but not for [role="button"]. The new span[role=button] elements don't receive focus-visible styling, creating inconsistency.
